verses.text: The LORD: take Aaron and his sons, the garments, the anointing oil, a bullock for sin offering, two rams, a basket of unleavened bread. Gather all the congregation at the tabernacle door. Moses did as commanded; the congregation gathered. Guidance: Aaron's ordination begins in public. The whole camp watches the priest become a priest. Holy callings are never hidden — God's people need to see the man set apart for them.

Moses brought Aaron and his sons, washed them. Put the coat on Aaron, girded him, clothed him with the robe, ephod, curious girdle. Put on the breastplate, Urim and Thummim. The mitre on his head; the golden plate, the holy crown — as the LORD commanded. Guidance: Washed first, then clothed layer upon layer until HOLINESS TO THE LORD shines from his forehead. The work is Moses' hands on Aaron's body; no one becomes a priest alone.

Moses took the anointing oil, anointed the tabernacle. Sprinkled the altar seven times, anointed vessels, the laver. Poured oil on Aaron's head. Brought his sons, clothed them in coats, girded them, put bonnets on them — as the LORD commanded. Guidance: The oil flows everywhere — building, altar, laver, priest. Psalm 133 calls this oil the picture of brotherhood under one anointing. What touches Aaron touches his sons, touches God's house. One anointing, many lives.

Brought the bullock for sin offering, Aaron and sons laid hands. Slew. Moses took blood, put on the altar's horns; poured rest at the base. Took fat, caul, kidneys — burnt on altar. The bullock burned outside the camp — as the LORD commanded. Guidance: Even Aaron — the priest-elect — needs a sin offering. No one ministers without first being atoned for. The altar itself is cleansed by the blood of the very animal Aaron's hands set apart.

Brought the ram for burnt offering, Aaron and sons laid hands. Slew. Moses sprinkled blood round the altar. Cut in pieces; burnt head, pieces, fat. Washed inwards; burnt the whole ram — sweet savor, fire offering — as the LORD commanded. Guidance: After sin is atoned, the priest is given wholly. The burnt offering is total surrender — every piece of the ram up in smoke. To be a priest is to belong entirely to the One you serve.

Brought the ram of consecration. Aaron and sons laid hands. Slew. Moses put blood on the right earlobe, thumb, big toe of Aaron and sons. Sprinkled rest round the altar. Took fat, kidneys, right shoulder, cakes. Waved on their hands; burnt. Took the breast, waved; Moses' part. Mixed oil with altar blood, sprinkled on Aaron, sons, garments. Guidance: Blood marks the priest's ear, thumb, toe. What he hears, does, goes — set apart. Oil-and-blood on garments seals it: priesthood paid for and Spirit-anointed at once.

Moses: boil the flesh at the door; eat with the bread of consecrations. What remains burn. Depart not from the door for seven days — He consecrates seven days. Abide day and night; keep the LORD's charge that ye die not. Aaron and sons did all the LORD commanded. Guidance: Seven days inside the door. The priest is shaped by waiting before he ministers. The same number of days God made the world is needed to make a priest. Holiness rests inside its own week.
